docs.title: Adding a Tutorial
docs.text:
To add a new tutorial:

  1. Create a Jupyter notebook (.ipynb) in the docs/tutorials/ directory
  2. Add it to the nav section in mkdocs.yml under Tutorials
  3. The notebook will be automatically rendered as a documentation page

Tip

Notebooks are rendered with mkdocs-jupyter. Cell outputs are included as-is (notebooks are not re-executed during the docs build). Make sure to run your notebook and save it with outputs before committing.

docs.title: Guidelines
docs.text:
• Use clear markdown cells to explain each step
  • Include expected outputs so readers can follow along without running the notebook
  • Keep notebooks focused on a single topic or workflow
  • Name files descriptively, e.g., basic_pipeline.ipynb, custom_evaluation.ipynb

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: NeMo Safe Synthesizer provides a command-line interface built with Click.

docs.title: Top-Level Commands
docs.text:
safe-synthesizer --help

Usage: safe-synthesizer [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  artifacts  Artifacts management commands.
  config     Manage Safe Synthesizer configurations.
  run        Run the Safe Synthesizer end-to-end pipeline.

docs.title: run -- Execute the Pipeline
docs.text: The run command executes the Safe Synthesizer pipeline. Without a subcommand, it runs the full end-to-end pipeline.

docs.title: Common Options
docs.text: Option Description Default --config TEXT Path to a YAML config file -- --url TEXT Dataset name, URL, or path to CSV -- --artifact-path DIR Base directory for all runs ./safe-synthesizer-artifacts --run-path DIR Explicit path for this run's output -- --output-file PATH Path to output CSV file -- --log-format [json\|plain] Console log format plain --wandb-mode [online\|offline\|disabled] WandB logging mode disabled -v / -vv Verbose logging --

docs.title: Subcommands
docs.text:
• run train -- Run only the training stage
  • run generate -- Run only the generation stage (requires a trained adapter)

docs.title: config -- Manage Configurations
docs.text:
safe-synthesizer config --help
Subcommand Description config validate Validate a configuration file config modify Modify a configuration file

docs.title: artifacts -- Artifacts Management
docs.text: safe-synthesizer artifacts --help

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description NSS_ARTIFACTS_PATH Default artifact path NSS_LOG_FORMAT Default log format NSS_LOG_FILE Default log file path NSS_DATASET_REGISTRY Dataset registry YAML path/URL WANDB_MODE WandB mode WANDB_PROJECT WandB project name WANDB_API_KEY WandB API key

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: NeMo Safe Synthesizer uses YAML configuration files validated by Pydantic models.

docs.title: Configuration File
docs.text:
Pass a YAML config file to the CLI:

safe-synthesizer run --config config.yaml --url data.csv

docs.title: Configuration Sections
docs.text: The top-level configuration (SafeSynthesizerParameters) aggregates the following sections:

docs.title: Data Parameters
docs.text: Controls dataset loading and preprocessing.

docs.title: Training Hyperparameters
docs.text: Controls LLM fine-tuning behavior.

docs.title: Generation Parameters
docs.text: Controls synthetic data generation.

docs.title: Evaluation Parameters
docs.text: Controls which evaluation components are enabled.

docs.title: PII Replacer Config
docs.text: Controls PII detection and replacement behavior.

docs.title: Differential Privacy Hyperparameters
docs.text: Controls DP-SGD training parameters.

docs.title: Dataset Registry
docs.text: Safe Synthesizer supports a dataset registry to simplify working with a standard set of datasets. Datasets in the registry can be referenced by name.

docs.title: Registry Format
docs.text:
base_url: /root/data/location
datasets:
  - name: dataset1
    url: dataset1.csv
  - name: dataset2
    url: dataset2.jsonl
    overrides:
      data:
        group_training_examples_by: id
  - name: dataset3
    url: https://myhost.com/path/to/dataset.json
    load_args:
      keyword: custom_arg_for_data_reader

Provide a registry via CLI (--dataset-registry) or environment variable (NSS_DATASET_REGISTRY).

docs.title: Evaluation
docs.text: Evaluation is a critical component of Safe Synthesizer that helps you understand both the utility and privacy of your synthetic data. The evaluation step is enabled by default and provides comprehensive reports comparing your original and synthetic datasets across multiple dimensions.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
The evaluation system compares your original and synthetic datasets using two main frameworks:

  1. Synthetic Quality Score (SQS): Measures how well the synthetic data preserves statistical properties and utility
  2. Data Privacy Score (DPS): Assesses privacy protection and resistance to various attack vectors

Each framework consists of multiple metrics that are combined into an overall score.

docs.title: Synthetic Quality Score (SQS)
docs.text: The SQS measures data utility across several dimensions:

docs.title: Column Correlation Stability
docs.text:
Analyzes the correlation across every combination of two columns:

  • Compares correlation matrices between original and synthetic data
  • Ensures relationships between variables are preserved
  • Critical for maintaining predictive power in ML models

docs.title: Deep Structure Stability
docs.text:
Uses Principal Component Analysis to reduce dimensionality when comparing datasets:

  • Captures overall data structure and patterns
  • Evaluates high-dimensional relationships
  • Assesses whether data maintains its fundamental characteristics

docs.title: Column Distribution Stability
docs.text:
Compares the distribution for each column in the original data to the matching column in the synthetic data:

  • Statistical tests for numeric columns (KS test, Wasserstein distance)
  • Frequency comparison for categorical columns
  • Identifies distribution drift

docs.title: Text Structure Similarity
docs.text:
For text columns, calculates sentence, word, and character counts:

  • Compares structural properties of text
  • Ensures text length and complexity are preserved
  • Validates text generation quality

docs.title: Text Semantic Similarity
docs.text:
Understands whether the semantic meaning of the text is maintained after synthesizing:

  • Uses embedding-based similarity measures
  • Captures contextual and semantic properties
  • Ensures text maintains intended meaning

docs.title: Data Privacy Score (DPS)
docs.text: The DPS assesses privacy protection through attack simulations:

docs.title: Membership Inference Protection
docs.text:
Tests whether attackers can determine if specific records were in the training data:

  • Simulates membership inference attacks
  • Measures how distinguishable training records are
  • Higher scores indicate better privacy protection

docs.title: Attribute Inference Protection
docs.text:
Assesses whether sensitive attributes can be inferred when other attributes are known:

  • Tests ability to predict hidden values
  • Measures information leakage
  • Validates that synthesis doesn't create inference vulnerabilities

docs.title: PII Replay
docs.text:
Evaluates the frequency with which sensitive values from the original data appear in the synthetic version:

  • Checks for exact matches of PII values
  • Identifies potential memorization
  • Critical for compliance and privacy guarantees

docs.title: Evaluation Reports
docs.text:
Every Safe Synthesizer run automatically generates an HTML evaluation report containing:

  • Overall SQS and DPS scores
  • Detailed subscores for each metric
  • Visualizations comparing original and synthetic data
  • Statistical test results

The report is saved to the run's generate/ directory as evaluation_report.html.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Evaluation is enabled by default but can be customized in your YAML config:

evaluation:
  mia_enabled: true   # Membership Inference Attack
  aia_enabled: true   # Attribute Inference Attack

docs.title: SQS Interpretation
docs.text: Score Range Rating Guidance 90-100 Excellent Synthetic data closely matches original utility 70-89 Good Suitable for most use cases with minor differences 50-69 Fair Noticeable differences, may impact some analyses Below 50 Poor Significant utility loss, review configuration

docs.title: DPS Interpretation
docs.text: Score Range Rating Guidance 90-100 Excellent Strong privacy protection 70-89 Good Adequate privacy for most use cases 50-69 Fair Some privacy risks, consider enabling differential privacy Below 50 Poor Insufficient privacy protection

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
• Evaluator
  • EvaluationParameters

docs.title: Pipeline Overview
docs.text: NeMo Safe Synthesizer enables you to create private versions of sensitive tabular datasets. The resulting data is entirely synthetic, with no one-to-one mapping to your original records. It is purpose-built for privacy compliance and data protection while preserving data utility for downstream AI tasks.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
Safe Synthesizer employs a novel approach to synthetic data generation:

  1. Tabular Fine-Tuning: Fine-tunes a language model on your tabular data to learn patterns, correlations, and statistical properties
  2. Generation: Uses the fine-tuned model to generate new synthetic records that maintain data utility
  3. Privacy Protection: Optionally applies differential privacy during training for mathematical privacy guarantees

docs.title: Pipeline Stages
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    data[("Input Data")]
    data --> pii{"Optional PII Replacement"}
    pii --> assemble("Assemble Examples")
    assemble --> train("Fine-tune LLM")
    train --> generate["Generate Samples"]
    generate --> evaluate["Evaluate"]

docs.title: 1. Data Preparation
docs.text:
The pipeline begins by loading your input data (CSV or DataFrame) and preparing it for training:

  • Data validation and preprocessing
  • Column type inference
  • Grouping and ordering (if configured)
  • Train/test split for holdout evaluation

docs.title: 2. PII Replacement (Optional)
docs.text:
If enabled, the PII replacer detects personally identifiable information using NER models and regex patterns, then replaces detected entities with synthetic but realistic values. This ensures the model has no chance of learning the most sensitive information like names and addresses.

See Privacy for detailed PII documentation.

docs.title: 3. Example Assembly
docs.text: Records are converted to a JSON format and tokenized for model training. The assembler handles truncation, padding, and proper formatting for the target LLM.

docs.title: 4. Training
docs.text:
The training stage fine-tunes a base LLM using LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation). Two backends are available:

Backend Description HuggingFace Standard training with quantization (4-bit/8-bit), LoRA via PEFT, and optional differential privacy via Opacus Unsloth Optimized training for faster fine-tuning

docs.title: 5. Generation
docs.text: Synthetic records are generated using the VLLM backend for fast inference. The generation stage loads the base model with the trained LoRA adapter and produces structured output.

docs.title: 6. Evaluation
docs.text: The evaluation stage computes privacy and quality metrics, then generates an HTML report with interactive visualizations. See Evaluation for details on all metrics.

docs.title: Supported Data Types
docs.text:
Safe Synthesizer supports diverse tabular data:

  • Numeric: Continuous and discrete numerical values
  • Categorical: Text labels and categories
  • Text: Free-form text fields
  • Temporal: Event sequences and time series

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
# Full end-to-end pipeline
safe-synthesizer run --config config.yaml --url data.csv

# Training only
safe-synthesizer run train --config config.yaml --url data.csv

# Generation only (requires a trained adapter)
safe-synthesizer run generate --config config.yaml --url data.csv --run-path /path/to/trained/run

docs.title: Python SDK
docs.text:
from nemo_safe_synthesizer.sdk.library_builder import SafeSynthesizer
from nemo_safe_synthesizer.config import SafeSynthesizerParameters

config = SafeSynthesizerParameters.from_yaml("config.yaml")
synthesizer = (
    SafeSynthesizer(config)
    .with_data_source("data.csv")
    .with_train(learning_rate=0.0005)
    .with_generate(num_records=5000)
    .with_evaluate(enabled=True)
)
synthesizer.run()
results = synthesizer.results

docs.title: Resource Planning
docs.text:
• Larger datasets and models require more GPU memory (80GB+ VRAM recommended)
  • Training time scales with data size and model complexity
  • Plan for 15-60 minutes for typical jobs
  • Ensure sufficient disk space for models and datasets (50GB+ recommended)

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
• Start with default settings
  • Enable PII replacement for sensitive data
  • Use differential privacy for maximum privacy guarantees
  • Adjust generation parameters based on evaluation results

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
GPU Memory Issues

  • Reduce batch_size in training parameters
  • Use a smaller subset of data for initial testing
  • Increase gradient_accumulation_steps to maintain effective batch size with lower memory
  • Check GPU usage with nvidia-smi

Invalid Data Format Errors

  • Ensure CSV is UTF-8 encoded
  • Validate column names don't contain special characters
  • Check for null values or inconsistent data types

Generation Quality Issues

  • Increase training data or epochs for more training
  • Adjust temperature (try 0.7-1.0 range)
  • Enable structured generation for better format adherence
  • Review evaluation report for specific quality issues

docs.title: Privacy
docs.text: NeMo Safe Synthesizer provides multiple layers of privacy protection for synthetic data generation.

docs.title: PII Replacement
docs.text: PII (Personally Identifiable Information) replacement is a critical privacy protection step that detects and replaces sensitive information in your datasets before synthesis. This ensures that the model has no chance of learning the most sensitive information like names, addresses, and other identifiers.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
The PII replacement pipeline operates in multiple stages:

  1. Detection: Identifies PII entities using configurable detection methods
  2. Classification: Categorizes detected entities by type (name, email, address, etc.)
  3. Transformation: Replaces or redacts PII using configurable rules
  4. Validation: Verifies that sensitive information has been properly handled

docs.title: Detection Methods
docs.text:
Safe Synthesizer supports multiple PII detection approaches:

Method Best For Speed Accuracy GLiNER General PII detection Fast High LLM Classification Context-aware, custom entities Slow Very High Regex Structured patterns (SSN, phone) Very Fast Perfect (for known formats)

docs.title: GLiNER Detection
docs.text:
Uses the GLiNER model for entity recognition:

  • Zero-shot entity detection
  • Supports custom entity types
  • High accuracy for standard PII categories
  • Configurable confidence thresholds

docs.title: LLM Classification
docs.text:
Leverages language models for PII detection:

  • Contextual understanding of entities
  • Handles complex PII patterns
  • Flexible entity definitions
  • Configurable prompts and models

docs.title: Regex Detection
docs.text:
Pattern-based detection for structured PII:

  • Fast and deterministic
  • Ideal for known formats (SSN, phone numbers)
  • Customizable patterns
  • Low computational overhead

docs.title: Replacement Strategies
docs.text:
After detection, PII can be handled in multiple ways:

  • Fake Data: Generate realistic replacements using the Faker library
  • Redaction: Replace with placeholder tokens
  • Hashing: One-way hashing for consistency
  • Custom Rules: Define your own transformation logic

docs.title: Supported Entity Types
docs.text:
Safe Synthesizer recognizes many PII types out of the box:

  • Personal: Names, dates of birth, ages
  • Contact: Emails, phone numbers, addresses
  • Identifiers: SSN, passport numbers, license numbers
  • Financial: Credit card numbers, bank accounts, IBAN, SWIFT codes
  • Medical: Patient IDs, medical record numbers
  • Digital: IP addresses, URLs, UUIDs, API keys, JWTs
  • Custom: Define your own entity types

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
PII replacement is configured through the replace_pii section of your YAML config:

enable_replace_pii: true
replace_pii:
  globals:
    locales:
      - en_US
  steps:
    - rows:
        update:
          - entity:
              - email
              - phone_number
            value: "column.entity | fake"

docs.title: When to Use PII Replacement
docs.text:
Consider using PII replacement when:

  • Your data contains names, addresses, or other direct identifiers
  • Compliance requires PII removal before processing
  • You want to ensure the model cannot memorize sensitive values
  • You need to share synthetic data with external parties

PII replacement can be used standalone (without synthesis) or as a preprocessing step before synthesis.

docs.title: Differential Privacy
docs.text: Safe Synthesizer supports Differential Privacy (DP) training via Opacus integration. DP provides mathematical guarantees that synthetic data doesn't reveal information about individual records in the training data.

docs.title: Key Concepts
docs.text:
• Epsilon (epsilon): Privacy budget -- lower values mean stronger privacy
    • epsilon = 1: Very strong privacy
    • epsilon = 6-10: Moderate privacy
    • epsilon > 10: Weak privacy
  • Delta (delta): Probability of privacy guarantee failure. Typically set to 1/n^2 where n is the dataset size (e.g., 1e-5)
  • Max Gradient Norm: Maximum gradient clipping norm, controls the sensitivity of the training process

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
DP-SGD (Differentially Private Stochastic Gradient Descent) adds calibrated noise to gradients during training, providing a mathematical privacy guarantee that limits what can be learned about any individual record:

  1. Gradient Clipping: Per-sample gradients are clipped to a maximum norm
  2. Noise Injection: Calibrated Gaussian noise is added to the clipped gradients
  3. Privacy Accounting: The total privacy budget (epsilon) is tracked across training

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Enable differential privacy in your YAML config:

differential_privacy:
  dp_enabled: true
  epsilon: 8.0
  delta: 1e-5
  per_sample_max_grad_norm: 1.0

Or via the Python SDK:

synthesizer = (
    SafeSynthesizer(config)
    .with_data_source("data.csv")
    .with_train(learning_rate=0.0005)
    .with_differential_privacy(dp_enabled=True, epsilon=8.0, delta=1e-5)
    .with_generate(num_records=5000)
)
synthesizer.run()

docs.title: Choosing Epsilon
docs.text: Sensitivity Recommended Epsilon Use Case High (medical, financial) 1.0 - 3.0 Maximum privacy, some utility loss Medium (general business) 3.0 - 6.0 Good balance of privacy and utility Low (public-adjacent data) 6.0 - 10.0 Better utility, moderate privacy

docs.title: Privacy Budget Management
docs.text:
Warning

Privacy budget compounds across releases. Each additional synthetic dataset released from the same original data increases the total privacy cost.

Best practices:

  1. Single Release: Only release one synthetic dataset per original dataset when possible
  2. Composition: If multiple releases are needed, divide the privacy budget accordingly
  3. Documentation: Track all data releases and cumulative privacy budget
  4. Renewal: Privacy budget doesn't reset -- consider this in your data lifecycle

docs.title: Privacy Evaluation
docs.text:
After generation, the evaluation system measures privacy through several metrics:

  • PII Replay Detection -- Checks if original PII values appear in synthetic data
  • Membership Inference Protection -- Measures resistance to membership inference attacks (can an attacker tell if a specific record was in the training data?)
  • Attribute Inference Protection -- Measures resistance to attribute inference attacks (can sensitive attributes be inferred from other known attributes?)

See Evaluation for full details on privacy and quality metrics.

docs.title: Python SDK
docs.text: The Python SDK provides a programmatic interface for building and running Safe Synthesizer workflows using a fluent builder pattern.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
from nemo_safe_synthesizer.sdk.library_builder import SafeSynthesizer
from nemo_safe_synthesizer.config import SafeSynthesizerParameters

# Load config from YAML
config = SafeSynthesizerParameters.from_yaml("config.yaml")

# Build and run the pipeline
synthesizer = SafeSynthesizer(config).with_data_source("data.csv")
synthesizer.run()

# Access results
results = synthesizer.results

docs.title: Builder Pattern
docs.text:
The SDK uses a fluent builder pattern for configuration:

synthesizer = (
    SafeSynthesizer(config)
    .with_data_source(df)
    .with_train(learning_rate=0.0001)
    .with_generate(num_records=10000)
    .with_evaluate(enabled=True)
)

docs.title: Step-by-Step Execution
docs.text:
For finer control, call individual pipeline stages:

synthesizer = SafeSynthesizer(config).with_data_source(df)

# Run stages individually
synthesizer.process_data()
synthesizer.train()
synthesizer.generate()
synthesizer.evaluate()

docs.title: Key Classes
docs.text: Class Description SafeSynthesizer Main entry point for SDK workflows ConfigBuilder Base builder for configuration management JobBuilder Builder for job-based workflows

docs.text:
For complete class and method documentation, see:
